briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Council to Approve Housing Agreement for 388-Unit Rental Development at 6616 Telford Avenue
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw (Bylaw No. 14790) that would authorize a formal housing agreement with the developer of a new rental building at 6616 Telford Avenue in Burnaby. If approved, the agreement would lock in rules for 304 below-market rental units and 84 market-rate rental units — setting rent limits, tenant eligibility requirements, and long-term affordability conditions that would run with the property permanently.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council adoption vote — this agreement will permanently affect rental rules at 6616 Telford Avenue.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Housing Agreement Bylaw 14790 — council adoption vote pending

briefs.industries: Landlord & Property Mgmt, Construction & Development, Tenant

briefs.title: Council to vote on: Bosa Properties housing agreement for 94 non-market rentals at 5980 Kathleen Ave
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would authorize a formal housing agreement with Bosa Properties (Metrotown) Inc. for a 35-storey rental building at 5980 Kathleen Avenue. The agreement would lock in 94 below-market rental units — 92 reserved for tenants displaced from prior sites, and 2 for income-qualifying households — with rents and tenant protections governed by Burnaby's Tenant Assistance Policy.
briefs.action_required: If you own or manage rental property near this site, review how Burnaby's Tenant Assistance Policy may affect your obligations.
briefs.industries: Landlord & Property Mgmt, Construction & Development, Tenant

briefs.title: Council to vote on final approval of purpose-built rental townhouse rezoning in Burnaby
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on the final adoption of a rezoning bylaw (Bylaw 14590) that would officially change the land use rules for several properties on Bevan Street and Marine Drive in the Edmonds area. If approved, the rezoning would allow Mosaic Avenue Developments to move forward with 'Bevan Townhomes,' a purpose-built rental townhouse project on what is described as a City-owned non-market housing site.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — this rezoning could affect nearby property values and rental housing supply in the Edmonds area.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Bylaw 14590 rezoning final adoption — council vote date

briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Tenant

briefs.title: Council to vote on: Rezoning approval for Bevan Townhomes development in Burnaby
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on final adoption of a bylaw that would rezone a parcel of land to allow the 'Bevan Townhomes' project, a townhouse development designed by Ekistics Architecture. The bylaw has already passed three readings and a public hearing — final adoption (the last formal vote needed to make it official) is now on the agenda for Council's consideration.
briefs.action_required: Check if your property or business is near this rezoned site — it may affect your neighbourhood.

briefs.title: Council to vote on Burnaby's proposed 2026–2030 Five-Year Financial Plan
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would adopt the city's five-year financial plan covering 2026 to 2030. If approved, the plan would set out how the city expects to raise and spend money each year — starting with roughly $873 million in revenue and $1.35 billion in total spending in 2026 — with property taxes making up the largest share of revenue at about 45%, and businesses carrying 37.1% of the property tax burden.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council vote — this plan sets property tax rates that will directly affect your business costs.
briefs.industries: Landlord & Property Mgmt, Construction & Development, Retail

briefs.title: Proposed: Rezoning of 1030 Sperling Ave, Burnaby to Allow 'Sperling Court' Development
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw (Bylaw No. 14791) that would rezone the property at 1030 Sperling Ave in Burnaby. If approved, the land would be redesignated — meaning the rules about what can be built there would change — to allow a new development called 'Sperling Court,' designed by Kennith Kim Architecture Inc.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council vote — this rezoning could affect nearby property values or development activity.

briefs.title: Council to consider: Burnaby's 2025 open resolutions and bylaw progress report
briefs.summary: At its January 27, 2026 meeting, Burnaby City Council is set to receive a status update on all 2025 council resolutions and bylaws — tracking which city-directed actions are completed, still in progress, or outstanding from 2024. The report also signals a change in how often these updates are shared: starting in 2026, progress reports will be published twice a year instead of quarterly.
briefs.action_required: Review the tracking index for any resolutions affecting your business or property and monitor the next update due mid-2026.

key_dates.label: Burnaby council resolutions progress report — January 2026 meeting

briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Professional Services

briefs.title: Council to Consider Approving Burnaby's 2026 Child Care Resource Group Workplan
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on endorsing the 2026 workplan for the Child Care Resources Group (CCRG) — an advisory body that guides the City on child care matters. Council will also consider approving $3,000 in funding to support a Child Care Provider Appreciation event and professional development session planned for May 2026.
briefs.action_required: If you run a child care facility in Burnaby, watch for the council decision — it may affect approval processes.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Child Care Provider Appreciation Event — CCRG

briefs.title: Council to vote on adding anti-Palestinian racism to Burnaby's Anti-Racism Framework
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on whether to officially recognize anti-Palestinian racism (APR) as a distinct form of racism and add its definition to the existing Burnaby Anti-Racism Framework. The definition, developed by the Arab Canadian Lawyers Association, describes APR as a form of anti-Arab racism that silences, excludes, stereotypes, or dehumanizes Palestinians or their stories.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — it may affect how your workplace anti-racism policies are expected to align with City standards.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council vote — anti-Palestinian racism added to Anti-Racism Framework

briefs.title: Council to vote on $240,000 contract renewal for Burnaby recyclable materials hauling
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on renewing a contract with GFL Environmental Inc. to haul recyclable materials from City facilities to an approved recycling depot. The proposed one-year renewal would run from February 1, 2026 to January 31, 2027, at an estimated cost of $240,000 including taxes — bringing the total contract value since 2022 to roughly $1.08 million.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the January 27, 2026 council decision if your business uses Burnaby recycling or hauling services.

key_dates.label: Burnaby recyclable materials hauling contract renewal — proposed start date
Burnaby recyclable materials hauling contract renewal — proposed end date

briefs.title: Council to Vote on $123,475 Contract Change for Burnaby Sewer Monitoring Sensors
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on approving a contract change worth $123,475 (including taxes) with ADS Environmental Technologies Inc. This proposed change would cover 45 replacement battery packs, 20 high-gain antennas, and 12 months of wireless monitoring subscriptions for the city's 45 sewer water level sensors — devices that detect blockages and help prevent sewer overflows across Burnaby.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision on January 27, 2026 — this affects city sewer infrastructure contracts.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council vote — sewer sensor contract change approval
Burnaby 2026–2030 Financial Plan council approval meeting
Contract Renewal #1 expiry — ADS sewer sensor contract ends

briefs.title: Council to vote on $1.04M contract for Burnaby Village Museum meadow and plaza upgrades
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on awarding a construction contract worth approximately $1,039,028 (including taxes) to Holaco Construction (1997) Ltd. The work would upgrade the meadow and Tram Barn Plaza at Burnaby Village Museum — adding a paved plaza surface, power and water hookups for events and food trucks, better drainage, lighting, and improved access between the meadow and the carousel building.
briefs.action_required: If your business supplies events or food trucks at Burnaby Village Museum, watch for the council decision on Jan 27, 2026.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council vote — Burnaby Village Museum contract award
Burnaby 2026–2030 Financial Plan approval — funding this project

briefs.title: Council to vote on $3.8M security system upgrade for Burnaby city facilities
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on awarding a $3.8 million contract to Paladin Technologies Inc. to upgrade and unify security systems across city facilities. The five-year deal (February 2026 – January 2031) would cover new cameras, access control systems, and ongoing maintenance — consolidating separate security setups into one city-wide platform.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision on January 27 — this contract affects security technology suppliers and installers.

key_dates.label: Burnaby facility security contract start date — Paladin Technologies agreement takes effect
Burnaby 2026–2030 Financial Plan operating budget — council approval expected

briefs.title: Council to vote on $1.3M traffic control services contract with The Universal Group
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on awarding a contract worth up to $1,300,000 (including taxes) to The Universal Group for flagging and traffic control services. If approved, the contract would run from February 1, 2026 to January 31, 2027, with up to four one-year renewal options.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision if you compete for city traffic control contracts.

key_dates.label: Burnaby flagging & traffic control contract start date — if approved
Burnaby Council vote on Universal Group contract award

briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Transportation & Logistics, Professional Services

briefs.title: Council to vote on $1.3M contract for flagging and traffic control services
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on awarding a contract worth up to $1,300,000 (including taxes) to Metro Traffic Ltd. for flagging and traffic control services. If approved, the contract would run from February 1, 2026 to January 31, 2027, with up to four one-year renewal options, covering road safety work tied to city construction, maintenance, and emergency responses.
briefs.action_required: If you provide traffic control or flagging services, monitor this contract award — it affects future bidding opportunities.

key_dates.label: Metro Traffic Ltd. flagging contract proposed start date
Metro Traffic Ltd. flagging contract initial term end date

briefs.title: Council to vote on $813,540 contract to replace aging air handling unit at Eileen Dailly Pool
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on awarding a contract worth $813,540 (including taxes) to Fortius Mechanical Inc. to replace the old air handling unit — the equipment that circulates and ventilates air — at the Eileen Dailly Leisure Pool & Fitness Centre. The unit has reached the end of its lifespan and must be replaced to keep the lobby and fitness areas properly ventilated.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision on January 27, 2026 — this is a public facility contract award.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council vote — Eileen Dailly Pool AHU1 contract award
Burnaby 2026–2030 Financial Plan approval — funding for this project

briefs.title: Council to vote on $2.625M contract for Burnaby outdoor pool tank repairs
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on awarding a $2,625,000 contract to ETRO Construction Limited to manage and carry out repairs on the city's aging outdoor pool tanks. The work — starting with Central Park Outdoor Pool in 2026–2027 — would include sandblasting, concrete crack fixes, pipe relining, and recoating, with three other pools (Kensington Park, MacPherson Park, and Robert Burnaby) scheduled in later phases.
briefs.action_required: If you bid on public construction contracts, watch for future RFPs as later pool phases are approved.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council vote — outdoor pool tank rehabilitation contract award
Burnaby 2026–2030 Financial Plan approval — funding for pool program

briefs.title: Council to vote on $5.75M contract for Burnaby fire station infrastructure upgrades
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on awarding a $5.75 million contract (including taxes) to Holaco Construction (1997) Ltd. to upgrade critical infrastructure at two Burnaby fire stations. The work would replace aging underground fuel tanks with above-ground fueling systems and install new emergency generators at Fire Station No. 1 and Fire Station No. 2 — keeping both stations fully operational during construction.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — this contract award may create subcontracting or supply opportunities.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council vote — Emergency Facilities Infrastructure Upgrades contract award

briefs.title: Council to vote on cost-sharing agreement with Burnaby School District 41 for 2026 election
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on allowing the City's Chief Election Officer to sign a cost-sharing agreement with Burnaby School District 41. Under the proposed deal, the City would run the School Trustee election on the School District's behalf — as it has done in past elections — with the School District pitching in by providing school buildings as voting locations and staff time to help offset costs.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — this affects how the October 2026 local election is run in Burnaby.

key_dates.label: Burnaby 2026 General Local Election day

briefs.title: Council to Consider Sending Feedback on BC's Speculation and Vacancy Tax Program
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on submitting a formal response to the BC Ministry of Finance as part of the province's annual consultation on the Speculation and Vacancy Tax (SVT) — a tax on empty or underused homes. The proposed response would ask the province to share SVT revenue directly with municipalities like Burnaby, and to give Burnaby the power to run its own empty homes tax, similar to what Vancouver already does.
briefs.action_required: If you own vacant or investment property in Burnaby, note that SVT rates are rising in 2026 — review your obligations now.

key_dates.label: BC Speculation and Vacancy Tax rate increase takes effect — all applicable properties

briefs.industries: Landlord & Property Mgmt, Finance & Insurance, Construction & Development

briefs.title: Council to vote on $195M loan guarantee bylaws for two Burnaby affordable housing projects
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on two proposed bylaws that would allow the City to back — that is, guarantee repayment of — loans totalling $195M for the Burnaby Housing Authority Corporation (BHA). The BHA is applying for a $100M loan for a housing project at 10th & Newcombe and a $95M loan for a project in Edmonds at Marine & Bevan, together delivering 386 non-market housing units. The bylaws are a precaution: city staff do not expect the City will ever need to borrow, but the approvals would be in place if the lender (CMHC) ever called on the guarantee.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — if approved, these guarantees could affect the City's future borrowing capacity and property tax levels.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council meeting — loan authorization bylaws vote

briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Finance & Insurance

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Council to Vote on Bike Learning Zone Location and Grant Funding
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on approving 7679 18th Street (in the Edmonds area) as the site for a new Bike Learning Zone — a safe, car-free space where children can learn to ride and practice road safety. Council will also consider directing staff to apply for outside grant funding to help pay for the design and construction, estimated at roughly $350,000.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — grant applications and construction timelines depend on this vote.

key_dates.label: ICBC Community Grants Program application deadline — Bike Learning Zone

briefs.title: Council to vote on converting 35 affordable rental units to market-rate at 5227 Lane Street
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on a developer's request to convert 35 below-market rental units (priced at CMHC median rates — a federally set affordability benchmark) into regular market-rate rentals at a new 70-unit building at 5227 Lane Street. The developer says rising construction costs and financing charges have made the project unviable without this change, and Council will consider repealing the existing Housing Agreement bylaw that locked in the affordable units.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the Council decision — if you own or manage rental property in Burnaby, this signals a shift in affordability requirements.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council vote — 5227 Lane Street affordable-to-market rental conversion

briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Finance & Insurance

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Council to Vote on 2026–2030 Five-Year Financial Plan and Bylaw
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on Burnaby's proposed 2026–2030 Financial Plan, which would set the city's spending and revenue rules for the next five years. If approved, the plan would introduce a 2.9% property tax increase, keep water rates flat, raise sewer rates by 3.5%, and maintain a 1.9% Growth Infrastructure Investment Levy — a special charge on property taxes to help pay for new community infrastructure tied to development.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council vote — the approved tax and utility rates will affect your 2026 property tax and utility bills.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Financial Plan Bylaw 2026 — expected council adoption date
Burnaby Tax Rates Bylaw 2026 — adoption deadline
Burnaby 2026 property tax bills — expected mailing to residents

briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail

briefs.title: Council to Consider New Non-Residential Zoning Districts Under Burnaby 2050 Plan
briefs.summary: At its January 13, 2026 meeting, Burnaby City Council reviewed several proposals with potential impacts on local businesses. Most notably, a proposed zoning bylaw rewrite would create five new non-residential zoning districts as part of the Burnaby 2050 land use plan — but Council sent it back to staff for more information after raising concerns about setbacks, unclear use definitions, and recycling facility rules.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the revised zoning bylaw report — it could affect where and how your business can operate in Burnaby.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail
